title: amaryllis × fulva
intro: Amaryllis × fulva, also known as the Belladonna Lily, is a hybrid of the amaryllis plant and a species of the lily family, characterized by its showy, funnel-shaped flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anticholinergic, antispasmodic, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Amaryllis × fulva include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as asthma and bronchitis, as well as its ability to relieve pain and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and dried flowers, which can be used to treat various health conditions, but caution is advised due to the plant's toxicity and potential side effects.
